disclaimer: For educational and research purposes only. Not medical advice. Consult a qualified healthcare professional before starting any protocol.

peptides.name: BPC-157
peptides.description: Synthetic 15-amino-acid peptide derived from gastric protein. One of the most researched tissue-repair peptides with demonstrated anti-inflammatory and healing properties in preclinical models.
peptides.primaryUse: Accelerates healing of muscles, tendons, ligaments, and gut tissue

peptides.name: TB-500
peptides.description: Synthetic peptide fragment of thymosin beta-4. Promotes angiogenesis, accelerates wound healing, and supports tissue regeneration through enhanced cell motility and blood vessel formation.
peptides.primaryUse: Promotes systemic tissue repair and reduces inflammation after injury

peptides.name: Ipamorelin
peptides.description: Selective growth hormone secretagogue (GHRP). Stimulates GH release without significantly elevating cortisol or ACTH. Excellent specificity profile among GH secretagogues.

dosing.notes: Inject on empty stomach. Best synergy with natural nocturnal GH pulse.

storage.notes: Allow to reach room temp before opening

peptides.primaryUse: Stimulates pulsatile growth hormone release with minimal side effects

peptides.name: CJC-1295 No DAC
peptides.description: Growth hormone releasing hormone (GHRH) analog. Short half-life (~30 min), mimics natural pulsatile GH release. Often stacked with Ipamorelin for synergistic GH stimulation.

dosing.notes: Stack with Ipamorelin for amplified GH release. Inject on empty stomach.

peptides.primaryUse: Short-acting GHRH analog that boosts GH pulses when combined with a GHRP

peptides.name: CJC-1295 DAC
peptides.description: Long-acting GHRH analog. DAC attachment extends half-life to ~8 days. Weekly or twice-weekly dosing. Maintains elevated GH/IGF-1 levels continuously.

storage.notes: Long half-life - less frequent injections required

peptides.primaryUse: Long-acting GHRH analog that provides sustained GH elevation

peptides.name: Sermorelin
peptides.description: First 29 amino acids of GHRH. FDA-approved form for pediatric GH deficiency. Stimulates natural GH release from pituitary. Most studied GHRH peptide with longest safety track record.
peptides.primaryUse: Stimulates natural GH production via the hypothalamic GHRH pathway

peptides.name: Tesamorelin
peptides.description: Synthetic analog of GHRH. FDA-approved for HIV-associated lipodystrophy. Strong evidence for visceral fat reduction and IGF-1 elevation.

storage.notes: FDA-approved - do not freeze lyophilized form

peptides.primaryUse: Reduces visceral fat and stimulates GH in HIV-associated lipodystrophy

peptides.name: GHRP-2
peptides.description: Second-generation GHRP with strong GH release. More potent than GHRP-6 with less hunger stimulation. Elevates cortisol and prolactin at higher doses.

dosing.notes: Up to 3x daily dosing possible. Inject on empty stomach for best GH pulse.

peptides.primaryUse: Potent GH secretagogue that also raises IGF-1 and appetite

peptides.name: GHRP-6
peptides.description: First-generation GHRP. Strong GH secretagogue with notable appetite stimulation (via ghrelin pathway). Good for bulking phases and GH optimization.

dosing.notes: Significant hunger stimulation. Plan meals accordingly.

peptides.primaryUse: Stimulates GH release and significantly increases appetite

peptides.name: IGF-1 LR3
peptides.description: Long-acting analog of IGF-1 with extended half-life (~20-30 hrs). Directly stimulates muscle growth, protein synthesis, and fat metabolism. Powerful anabolic compound.

dosing.notes: Short cycles recommended. Monitor blood glucose. Desensitization occurs with prolonged use.

reconstitution.notes: Use acetic acid for reconstitution, NOT BAC water

storage.notes: Very temperature sensitive. Most delicate peptide in this list.

peptides.primaryUse: Promotes muscle cell growth and recovery via IGF-1 receptor activation

peptides.name: MGF
peptides.description: Splice variant of IGF-1 expressed in response to muscle damage. Activates satellite cells for muscle repair and growth. Short half-life - best used post-exercise.
peptides.primaryUse: Activates satellite cells to repair and build muscle after exercise

peptides.name: Epitalon
peptides.description: Synthetic tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) based on epithalamin from pineal gland. Activates telomerase, may extend telomere length. One of most studied anti-aging peptides.

dosing.notes: Long break periods between cycles (6-12 months). Annual or biannual protocol is common.

peptides.primaryUse: Telomere-extending tetrapeptide that promotes longevity and anti-aging

peptides.name: Thymosin Alpha-1
peptides.description: Naturally occurring peptide derived from thymosin fraction 5. FDA-approved in multiple countries as Zadaxin® for hepatitis and immunodeficiency. Potent immune modulator.
peptides.primaryUse: Thymic peptide that enhances immune surveillance and antiviral defense

peptides.name: Semaglutide
peptides.description: GLP-1 receptor agonist with 94% homology to human GLP-1. Weekly injection. Approved as Ozempic® and Wegovy®. Potent weight loss and blood sugar regulation.

dosing.notes: Titrate slowly over months to minimize GI side effects. Maintain same day of week for injection.

peptides.primaryUse: GLP-1 receptor agonist that reduces appetite and promotes weight loss

peptides.name: Tirzepatide
peptides.description: Dual GLP-1 and GIP receptor agonist. Approved as Mounjaro® (T2D) and Zepbound® (obesity). Superior weight loss outcomes vs semaglutide in clinical trials.

dosing.notes: Escalate dose every 4 weeks. Maintain consistent weekly timing.

peptides.primaryUse: Dual GIP/GLP-1 agonist offering superior weight loss and glucose control

peptides.name: Retatrutide
peptides.description: Triple-receptor agonist (GLP-1, GIP, glucagon). Most potent weight loss peptide in trials. Phase 3 clinical trials showing ~24% body weight reduction.

dosing.notes: Escalate extremely slowly - 1mg/month increments. Most potent GLP agent.

peptides.primaryUse: Triple receptor agonist (GLP-1/GIP/glucagon) for aggressive weight reduction

peptides.name: AOD-9604
peptides.description: Fragment of HGH (amino acids 176-191) with fat-burning properties. No effect on blood sugar or growth. Originally developed by Monash University for obesity treatment.
peptides.primaryUse: Fat-burning peptide fragment that mimics the lipolytic region of HGH

peptides.name: PT-141
peptides.description: Melanocortin receptor agonist. FDA-approved as Vyleesi® for female sexual dysfunction. Also studied for male erectile dysfunction. Acts centrally on brain rather than vascular system.

dosing.notes: As-needed dosing. Not for daily use. Maximum 1x per 24 hours.

peptides.primaryUse: Melanocortin agonist that increases sexual arousal in men and women

peptides.name: Melanotan II
peptides.description: Synthetic analog of alpha-MSH. Stimulates melanogenesis (tanning), libido, and erectile function via melanocortin receptors. Non-specific melanocortin agonist.

dosing.notes: Start very low to assess nausea. Use sunscreen still required. Monitor moles.

peptides.primaryUse: Melanocortin peptide that enhances tanning, libido, and erections

peptides.name: GHK-Cu
peptides.description: Naturally occurring copper complex in human plasma. Potent wound healer, collagen stimulator, and anti-aging compound. Used topically and systemically.
peptides.primaryUse: Stimulates skin regeneration, wound healing, and collagen production
sideEffects: Mild skin irritation at injection site

peptides.name: DSIP
peptides.description: Endogenous neuropeptide that modulates sleep. Reduces stress hormones, promotes delta (deep) sleep, and has analgesic properties. Studied for stress and sleep disorders.
peptides.primaryUse: Delta sleep-inducing peptide that regulates sleep cycles and reduces stress

peptides.name: Selank
peptides.description: Synthetic analogue of tuftsin. Anxiolytic and nootropic peptide. Modulates GABA and serotonin systems. Russian-developed peptide with strong anxiolytic properties without sedation.
peptides.primaryUse: Reduces anxiety and enhances cognitive function via GABA modulation

peptides.name: Semax
peptides.description: Synthetic ACTH analog. Strong nootropic and neuroprotective effects. Increases BDNF and NGF. Used in Russia as approved drug for stroke and cognitive disorders.
peptides.primaryUse: Neuroprotective peptide that enhances memory, focus, and BDNF levels

peptides.name: PE-22-28
peptides.description: Spadin analog targeting TREK-1 potassium channel. Antidepressant mechanism distinct from SSRIs. Rapid onset mood improvement with potential for depression treatment.
peptides.primaryUse: Antidepressant peptide that promotes neuroplasticity and mood stability

peptides.name: NAD+
peptides.description: Essential coenzyme in energy metabolism. Declines with age. IV or SubQ supplementation replenishes cellular NAD+ for mitochondrial function, DNA repair, and sirtuin activation.

dosing.notes: SubQ can cause burning sensation. Inject very slowly. IV preferred for higher doses.

peptides.primaryUse: Restores cellular NAD+ levels to support energy, DNA repair, and aging

peptides.name: MOTS-C
peptides.fullName: MOTS-C (Mitochondrial ORF of the 12S rRNA Type-C)
peptides.description: Mitochondria-derived peptide. Regulates metabolic homeostasis, insulin sensitivity, and exercise capacity. Mimics exercise at the cellular level.
peptides.primaryUse: Mitochondrial-derived peptide that improves insulin sensitivity and metabolism

peptides.name: SS-31
peptides.description: Szeto-Schiller peptide that targets mitochondrial inner membrane. Improves mitochondrial efficiency, reduces ROS, and improves heart/kidney function in clinical trials.
peptides.primaryUse: Mitochondria-targeted antioxidant that protects against oxidative damage

peptides.name: AICAR
peptides.description: AMPK activator. Mimics exercise adaptation at cellular level. Improves endurance, insulin sensitivity, and fat oxidation. Called an 'exercise in a pill' compound.
peptides.primaryUse: AMPK activator that mimics exercise effects on metabolism and endurance

peptides.name: SLU-PP-332
peptides.description: Estrogen-related receptor (ERR) agonist. Activates genes involved in mitochondrial biogenesis and oxidative metabolism. Exercise-mimicking compound in early research.
peptides.primaryUse: Exercise mimetic that activates ERR receptors for metabolic and endurance gains

peptides.name: Gonadorelin
peptides.description: Synthetic GnRH (gonadotropin-releasing hormone). Stimulates LH and FSH release from pituitary. Used to maintain testicular function and fertility during TRT or post-cycle.
peptides.primaryUse: Mimics GnRH to stimulate LH/FSH and maintain testicular function

peptides.name: HCG
peptides.description: Mimics LH in the body. Stimulates testosterone production and maintains testicular size/function. Used during TRT and PCT. Measured in International Units (IU).
peptides.primaryUse: Mimics LH to support testosterone production and testicular size

peptides.name: HGH 191AA
peptides.description: Synthetic 191-amino acid growth hormone identical to natural human GH. The 'gold standard' of GH therapy. Measured in IU. Comprehensive anabolic and metabolic effects.

dosing.notes: Inject morning for fat loss benefits. Evening for GH pulse synergy. Avoid eating 1hr before/after.

reconstitution.notes: Extremely gentle reconstitution - inject BAC water down vial wall slowly

peptides.primaryUse: Replaces or supplements endogenous growth hormone for body composition

peptides.name: LL-37
peptides.description: Human cathelicidin antimicrobial peptide. Broad-spectrum antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and wound healing properties. Key component of innate immune defense.
peptides.primaryUse: Provides antimicrobial defense and modulates immune response

peptides.name: KPV
peptides.description: C-terminal tripeptide of alpha-MSH. Potent anti-inflammatory. Specifically targets gut inflammation. Excellent for inflammatory bowel conditions.
peptides.primaryUse: Reduces gut inflammation and supports intestinal barrier repair

peptides.name: Glutathione
peptides.description: Master antioxidant tripeptide. Neutralizes free radicals, supports liver detoxification, and brightens skin. Injectable form has superior bioavailability vs oral.
peptides.primaryUse: Master antioxidant that supports liver detox and cellular protection

peptides.name: 5-Amino-1MQ
peptides.description: NNMT inhibitor. Reactivates brown fat metabolism. Studied for obesity reversal without exercise. Stimulates NAD+ pathway and fat cell reprogramming.
peptides.primaryUse: NNMT inhibitor that boosts metabolism and promotes fat loss

peptides.name: FOXO4-DRI
peptides.description: Senolytic peptide that selectively induces apoptosis in senescent (zombie) cells. Clears cellular senescence, demonstrated tissue rejuvenation in mouse models.

dosing.notes: Short pulse cycles with very long breaks. Senolytic - clears cells, needs recovery time.

peptides.primaryUse: Senolytic peptide that clears senescent cells to restore tissue function

peptides.name: SNAP-8
peptides.description: SNAP-25 mimic that reduces muscle contraction. Topical anti-wrinkle peptide similar to Botox mechanism but milder. Reduces expression lines.
peptides.primaryUse: Reduces the appearance of expression lines and wrinkles

peptides.name: Cagrilintide
peptides.description: Long-acting amylin analog. Reduces appetite via brainstem pathways. In CagriSema trials (with semaglutide), showed ~24% weight reduction. Complementary to GLP-1 therapy.
peptides.primaryUse: Long-acting amylin analog that reduces appetite and body weight

peptides.name: Oxytocin
peptides.description: Neuropeptide known as the 'love hormone'. Promotes social bonding, reduces anxiety, lowers cortisol. Used for social anxiety, PTSD, and bonding disorders.
peptides.primaryUse: Bonding and trust hormone that reduces anxiety and enhances social connection

peptides.name: L-Carnitine
peptides.description: Amino acid derivative essential for fatty acid transport into mitochondria. Enhances fat oxidation, athletic performance, and recovery. Injectable form has superior absorption.
peptides.primaryUse: Shuttles fatty acids into mitochondria to support fat burning and energy

peptides.name: Kisspeptin
peptides.description: Hypothalamic neuropeptide that triggers GnRH release. Upstream regulator of the HPG axis. Used for fertility, testosterone support, and puberty disorders.
peptides.primaryUse: Hypothalamic peptide that stimulates GnRH to support reproductive hormones

peptides.name: Survodutide
peptides.description: Dual GLP-1 and glucagon receptor agonist. In Phase 3 trials for obesity and NASH. Glucagon component enhances liver fat clearance beyond GLP-1 alone.
peptides.primaryUse: GLP-1/glucagon dual agonist for weight loss and metabolic improvement

peptides.name: Mazdutide
peptides.description: GLP-1/glucagon receptor co-agonist from Innovent Biologics. Chinese-developed obesity treatment showing strong weight loss in Phase 3 trials. Similar profile to survodutide.
peptides.primaryUse: GLP-1/glucagon dual agonist with strong weight loss and glycemic control

peptides.name: 5-Amino-1MQ (50mg)
peptides.description: NNMT inhibitor. Reactivates brown fat metabolism. Studied for obesity reversal without exercise. Stimulates NAD+ pathway and fat cell reprogramming.
peptides.primaryUse: NNMT inhibitor that boosts metabolism and promotes fat loss

peptides.name: Tirzepatide (15mg)
peptides.description: Dual GLP-1 and GIP receptor agonist. Approved as Mounjaro® (T2D) and Zepbound® (obesity). Superior weight loss outcomes vs semaglutide in clinical trials.

dosing.notes: Escalate dose every 4 weeks. Maintain consistent weekly timing.

peptides.description: Dual GLP-1 and GIP receptor agonist. Approved as Mounjaro® (T2D) and Zepbound® (obesity). Superior weight loss outcomes vs semaglutide in clinical trials.

dosing.notes: Escalate dose every 4 weeks. Maintain consistent weekly timing.

peptides.primaryUse: Dual GIP/GLP-1 agonist offering superior weight loss and glucose control

peptides.name: Adamax (10 mg)
peptides.description: Adamax (Dihexa) is a highly potent nootropic peptide that crosses the blood-brain barrier and enhances cognitive function by potentiating HGF/MET signaling. Research suggests it is millions of times more potent than BDNF in forming new synaptic connections.
peptides.primaryUse: Nootropic peptide stack that supports memory, learning, and neuroprotection
peptides.notes: Extremely potent — start at the lowest dose. Half-life is very long. Begin weekly or every-other-day dosing before moving to daily. Research use only.

peptides.name: Adipotide (10 mg)
peptides.description: Adipotide (FTPP) is a proapoptotic peptide that selectively targets blood vessels supplying white adipose tissue. Research shows it induces apoptosis of adipose vasculature, leading to significant fat mass reduction in primate models.
peptides.primaryUse: Targets and destroys adipose tissue blood supply for localized fat loss

storage.notes: Keep cycles short — nephrotoxicity risk with extended use

peptides.notes: CAUTION: Significant nephrotoxicity risk. Keep cycles to 4-5 weeks maximum. Monitor kidney function (creatinine, BUN) throughout. Maintain high water intake. Research use only.

peptides.name: Ara-290 (16 mg)
peptides.description: Ara-290 is a non-hematopoietic erythropoietin analogue that selectively binds to innate repair receptors without stimulating red blood cell production. Clinical research shows neuroprotection, anti-inflammatory effects, and improved nerve conduction in small fiber neuropathy.
peptides.primaryUse: Erythropoietin analog that reduces neuropathic pain and inflammation
peptides.notes: Does not stimulate RBC production unlike standard EPO. Shown in clinical trials to improve small fiber neuropathy symptoms. Administer 3x/week (e.g., Mon/Wed/Fri).

peptides.name: Cartalax (20 mg)
peptides.description: Cartalax is a Russian peptide bioregulator derived from cartilage tissue. Contains the tetrapeptide Ala-Glu-Asp-Leu. Research shows it normalizes chondrocyte function, supports cartilage integrity, and may slow age-related joint degeneration.
peptides.primaryUse: Bioregulator that supports cartilage, connective tissue, and joint health
peptides.notes: Russian peptide bioregulator. Protocol: 10-day course (1mg/day SC), repeated 2-3 times per year. Allow at least 3 months between courses.

peptides.name: Cerebrolysin (60 mg)
peptides.description: Cerebrolysin is a complex mixture of neuropeptides and low-molecular-weight proteins derived from purified pig brain. Contains BDNF-like, CNTF-like, and GDNF-like factors. Extensive clinical research supports neuroprotection, cognitive enhancement, and recovery after neurological injury.
peptides.primaryUse: Neuroprotective growth factor blend supporting brain repair and cognition

storage.notes: Use within 14 days after reconstitution. May also come as pre-made solution.

peptides.notes: 5-day weekday courses (Mon-Fri), 20 sessions per cycle, 2-3 cycles/year. Administer slowly IM or diluted IV. Used in Eastern European clinical practice for decades.

peptides.name: Chonluten (20 mg)
peptides.description: Chonluten is a Russian peptide bioregulator for bronchi and lung tissue. Contains the tripeptide Lys-Glu-Asp. Research shows it normalizes bronchial epithelial cell gene expression and may support respiratory function and lung longevity.
peptides.primaryUse: Bronchial epithelial bioregulator that supports lung tissue health
peptides.notes: Russian peptide bioregulator. Protocol: 10-day course (1mg/day SC), repeated 2-3 times per year. Allow at least 3 months between courses.

peptides.name: Cortagen (20 mg)
peptides.description: Cortagen is a Russian peptide bioregulator for cardiac and myocardial tissue. Research shows it normalizes cardiomyocyte function and may support cardiovascular health and heart longevity.
peptides.primaryUse: Vascular and cardiac bioregulator targeting heart and vessel tissue
peptides.notes: Russian peptide bioregulator. Protocol: 10-day course (1mg/day SC), repeated 2-3 times per year. Allow at least 3 months between courses.

peptides.name: HMG (75 IU)
peptides.fullName: Human Menopausal Gonadotropin (FSH + LH, 75 IU each)
peptides.description: HMG is a purified gonadotropin preparation containing approximately equal amounts of FSH (follicle-stimulating hormone) and LH (luteinizing hormone). Used in fertility protocols to stimulate follicular development and ovulation in women, and to support testosterone production and spermatogenesis in men.
peptides.primaryUse: Stimulates follicle development in fertility protocols (FSH + LH activity)

storage.notes: Use within 28 days after reconstitution. Sensitive to heat and light.

peptides.notes: Use under medical supervision. Doses and frequency vary significantly based on clinical indication. Monitor estradiol and follicle development in women. Not for use without proper hormonal monitoring.

peptides.name: Livagen (20 mg)
peptides.description: Livagen is a Russian peptide bioregulator targeting liver and cardiovascular tissues. Contains the tetrapeptide Lys-Glu-Asp-Ala. Research shows it activates chromatin in liver cells and may support hepatic function and metabolic health.
peptides.primaryUse: Cardiovascular and liver bioregulator that supports vascular function
peptides.notes: Russian peptide bioregulator. Protocol: 10-day course (1mg/day SC), repeated 2-3 times per year. Allow at least 3 months between courses.

peptides.name: Ovagen (20 mg)
peptides.description: Ovagen is a Russian peptide bioregulator for liver function. Contains the tripeptide Glu-Asp-Leu. Research shows it normalizes hepatocyte function and supports liver regeneration and health.
peptides.primaryUse: Reproductive organ bioregulator supporting liver, kidney, and gut tissue
peptides.notes: Russian peptide bioregulator. Protocol: 10-day course (1mg/day SC), repeated 2-3 times per year. Allow at least 3 months between courses.

peptides.name: PEG MGF (2 mg)
peptides.description: PEG-MGF is a pegylated form of Mechano Growth Factor (MGF), an IGF-1 splice variant produced by mechanically stressed muscle. Pegylation extends half-life from minutes to days. Research shows it promotes muscle satellite cell activation, hypertrophy, and recovery.
peptides.primaryUse: Longer-acting MGF analog for sustained muscle repair and hypertrophy
peptides.notes: Unlike native MGF, PEG-MGF remains active for days so timing is less critical. Administer 3x/week (e.g., Mon/Wed/Fri). Often stacked with IGF-1 LR3 or GHRP/CJC protocols.

peptides.name: Pinealon (20 mg)
peptides.description: Pinealon is a Russian peptide bioregulator for the brain and pineal gland. Contains the tripeptide Ala-Glu-Asp. Research shows it supports pineal gland function, melatonin regulation, neuroprotection, and may improve sleep quality and cognitive function.
peptides.primaryUse: Pineal gland bioregulator that improves sleep quality and cognitive function
peptides.notes: Russian peptide bioregulator. Protocol: 10-day course (1mg/day SC), repeated 2-3 times per year. Allow at least 3 months between courses.

peptides.name: PNC-27 (30 mg)
peptides.description: PNC-27 is a research peptide containing the MDM2-binding domain of p53 fused to a penetratin cell-penetrating sequence. Research suggests selective disruption of cancer cell membranes while leaving normal cells unaffected. Currently in early-stage oncology research.
peptides.primaryUse: Anti-cancer peptide that selectively induces apoptosis in tumor cells

storage.notes: For research use only. Handle with care.

peptides.notes: Strictly research use only. Dosing protocols are not well established in humans. Consult primary literature before use.

peptides.name: Prostamax (20 mg)
peptides.description: Prostamax is a Russian peptide bioregulator for prostate tissue. Contains the tetrapeptide Lys-Asp-Glu-Leu. Research shows it normalizes prostate glandular cell function and may support men's prostate health and urinary function.
peptides.primaryUse: Prostate-specific bioregulator that supports gland health and function
peptides.notes: Russian peptide bioregulator. Protocol: 10-day course (1mg/day SC), repeated 2-3 times per year. Allow at least 3 months between courses.

peptides.name: Testagen (20 mg)
peptides.description: Testagen is a Russian peptide bioregulator for testicular tissue. Research shows it supports gonadal cell function and may help maintain testosterone production and reproductive health in aging men.
peptides.primaryUse: Thyroid and immune bioregulator that supports hormonal and immune balance
peptides.notes: Russian peptide bioregulator. Protocol: 10-day course (1mg/day SC), repeated 2-3 times per year. Allow at least 3 months between courses.

peptides.name: Vesugen (20 mg)
peptides.description: Vesugen is a Russian peptide bioregulator for vascular/blood vessel tissue. Contains the tripeptide Lys-Glu-Asp. Research shows it normalizes vascular endothelial cell function and supports cardiovascular health and blood vessel integrity.
peptides.primaryUse: Vascular system bioregulator that protects blood vessel integrity
peptides.notes: Russian peptide bioregulator. Protocol: 10-day course (1mg/day SC), repeated 2-3 times per year. Allow at least 3 months between courses.

peptides.name: Vilon (20 mg)
peptides.description: Vilon is a Russian dipeptide peptide bioregulator (Lys-Glu) derived from thymus tissue. Research shows it stimulates immune function, exhibits anti-aging properties, and may extend lifespan through immune system regulation.
peptides.primaryUse: Dipeptide immune bioregulator that modulates neuroendocrine-immune function
peptides.notes: Russian peptide bioregulator. Protocol: 10-day course (1mg/day SC), repeated 2-3 times per year. Derived from thymus. Often combined with Epithalon in longevity protocols.

peptides.name: BPC-157 + TB-500 (10mg Blend)
peptides.description: Pre-mixed single-vial blend combining BPC-157 and TB-500 for synergistic tissue repair. One injection delivers both peptides, targeting complementary healing pathways — BPC-157 for localized repair and TB-500 for systemic angiogenesis and cell migration.

dosing.notes: Total combined dose per injection. Each vial contains equal parts BPC-157 and TB-500.

peptides.primaryUse: Combined tissue repair and anti-inflammatory action for accelerated injury recovery

peptides.name: BPC-157 + TB-500 (20mg Blend)
peptides.description: Higher-dose pre-mixed blend of BPC-157 and TB-500 in a single 20mg vial. Designed for more intensive recovery protocols or longer cycles. Delivers both peptides in one injection for comprehensive tissue repair support.

dosing.notes: Total combined dose per injection from 20mg vial.

peptides.primaryUse: Combined tissue repair and anti-inflammatory action for accelerated injury recovery

peptides.name: CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin (10mg Blend)
peptides.fullName: CJC-1295 No DAC + Ipamorelin Pre-Mixed Blend (10mg Vial)
peptides.description: Pre-mixed blend of CJC-1295 (No DAC) and Ipamorelin, two complementary GH secretagogues. CJC-1295 stimulates GHRH receptors while Ipamorelin activates ghrelin receptors, together amplifying natural GH pulses. One daily injection before bed.

dosing.notes: Total combined dose (100-300 mcg each). Best administered before bed to align with nocturnal GH pulses.

peptides.primaryUse: Synergistic GH pulse amplification for muscle growth, fat loss, and recovery

peptides.name: AOD-9604 + CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin (12mg Blend)
peptides.fullName: AOD-9604 + CJC-1295 No DAC + Ipamorelin Tri-Blend (12mg Vial)
peptides.description: Triple-peptide blend combining AOD-9604 (fat metabolism), CJC-1295 No DAC (GHRH stimulation), and Ipamorelin (GH pulse trigger). One injection simultaneously supports fat loss, GH secretion, and body composition improvement.

dosing.notes: Standard dose: 200 mcg AOD + 100 mcg CJC + 100 mcg Ipamorelin = 400 mcg total. Up to 800 mcg total for aggressive dosing.

peptides.primaryUse: Fat loss plus GH optimization in a single multi-peptide blend

peptides.name: Cagrilintide + Semaglutide (10mg Blend)
peptides.description: Dual GLP-1/amylin receptor blend combining semaglutide (GLP-1 receptor agonist) and cagrilintide (long-acting amylin analog). Synergistic weight loss through complementary appetite suppression and metabolic pathways. Weekly subcutaneous injection.

dosing.notes: Weekly injection — typical dose is 0.25 mg each (total 0.5 mg) titrating up to 2.4 mg each. Long-term protocol.

titration.note: Up to 2.4 mg each at maximum

peptides.primaryUse: Dual-mechanism weight loss combining amylin and GLP-1 agonism

peptides.name: CJC-1295 + GHRP-2 (10mg Blend)
peptides.fullName: CJC-1295 No DAC + GHRP-2 Pre-Mixed Blend (10mg Vial)
peptides.description: Pre-mixed blend of CJC-1295 (No DAC) and GHRP-2, combining GHRH analog with a potent GH-releasing peptide. GHRP-2 is a stronger GH releaser than Ipamorelin with more robust pulses. Best timed in the evening before bed.

dosing.notes: Total combined dose (75-150 mcg CJC + 75-150 mcg GHRP-2). Evening dosing recommended for nocturnal GH pulse amplification.

peptides.primaryUse: Powerful GH release stack for body recomposition and anti-aging

peptides.name: GLOW (70mg Blend)
peptides.fullName: GLOW Blend — GHK-Cu + TB-500 + BPC-157 (70mg Vial)
peptides.description: GLOW is a 70mg cosmetic and tissue-repair blend containing GHK-Cu (copper peptide for collagen and skin), TB-500 (angiogenesis and healing), and BPC-157 (tissue repair). Targeted at skin rejuvenation, anti-aging, and systemic healing support.

dosing.notes: Combined daily dose. Short cycles (4 weeks) with 2-4 week breaks recommended.

storage.notes: GHK-Cu is light-sensitive; store in dark container

peptides.primaryUse: Skin, hair, and nail enhancement through combined regenerative peptides

peptides.name: KLOW (80mg Blend)
peptides.fullName: KLOW Blend — TB-500 + BPC-157 + KPV + GHK-Cu (80mg Vial)
peptides.description: KLOW is an 80mg quad-peptide blend combining TB-500, BPC-157, KPV (anti-inflammatory tripeptide), and GHK-Cu (copper peptide). Designed for comprehensive tissue repair, inflammation control, and skin health in one daily injection.

dosing.notes: Total combined dose. Standard: 500 mcg each of TB-500, BPC-157, KPV, and GHK-Cu per injection.

storage.notes: Protect from light; avoid freeze-thaw after reconstitution

peptides.primaryUse: Multi-target weight loss blend addressing appetite, metabolism, and fat

peptides.name: Neuroxelin (48mg Blend)
peptides.description: Neuroxelin is a 48mg nootropic peptide blend targeting cognitive enhancement, neuroprotection, and neuroplasticity. Contains cognitive-focused peptides including Semax, Selank, and related compounds. Best administered in the morning.

dosing.notes: Morning administration recommended to maximize cognitive benefits throughout the day. Avoid evening use.

peptides.primaryUse: Cognitive and neuroprotective blend supporting brain health and focus

peptides.name: Tesamorelin + Ipamorelin (10mg Blend)
peptides.description: FDA-referenced peptide blend combining Tesamorelin (GHRH analog approved for visceral adiposity) with Ipamorelin (selective GH secretagogue). Synergistic GH stimulation with focus on visceral fat reduction, lean mass, and metabolic health.

dosing.notes: Gradual titration: start 250 mcg Tesamorelin + 125 mcg Ipamorelin, titrate up. Max: 2000 mcg Tesamorelin + 1000 mcg Ipamorelin.

peptides.primaryUse: Targeted visceral fat reduction combined with GH pulse optimization

peptides.name: Tri-Heal (45mg Blend)
peptides.fullName: Tri-Heal Blend — TB-500 + BPC-157 + KPV (45mg Vial)
peptides.description: Tri-Heal is a 45mg triple-peptide recovery blend containing TB-500 (systemic healing), BPC-157 (tissue repair), and KPV (anti-inflammatory tripeptide). Designed for accelerated musculoskeletal recovery and injury support.

dosing.notes: Combined total dose of all three peptides. Inject near the area of injury for enhanced localized effect.

peptides.primaryUse: Triple-action tissue repair blend for musculoskeletal injury recovery

peptides.name: CJC-1295 DAC (2mg) + Ipamorelin (5mg) Stack
peptides.fullName: CJC-1295 DAC 2mg Vial + Ipamorelin 5mg Vial Stack Protocol
peptides.description: Two-vial stack combining CJC-1295 DAC (long-acting GHRH analog, weekly injection) with Ipamorelin (selective GH secretagogue, daily injection). Complementary pathways amplify endogenous GH and IGF-1. The scheduler tracks the daily Ipamorelin injection; CJC-1295 DAC is taken once per week separately.

dosing.notes: Dose shown is Ipamorelin (daily). CJC-1295 DAC: 1000 mcg (1mg) once weekly, fixed day. Ipamorelin titration: 100 mcg wks 1-2 → 150 mcg wks 3-4 → 200 mcg wks 5+.

reconstitution.notes: Each vial reconstituted separately. CJC-1295 DAC: 2mL BW (1mg/mL). Ipamorelin: 3mL BW (1.67mg/mL).

storage.notes: Store vials separately; do not pre-mix

peptides.primaryUse: Entry-level GH optimization stack balancing pulse and sustained release

peptides.name: CJC-1295 DAC (5mg) + Ipamorelin (5mg) Stack
peptides.fullName: CJC-1295 DAC 5mg Vial + Ipamorelin 5mg Vial Stack Protocol
peptides.description: Two-vial stack using a larger 5mg CJC-1295 DAC vial paired with Ipamorelin. Functionally identical to the 2mg version but the 5mg vial provides more doses before reconstitution is needed. Weekly CJC-1295 DAC + daily Ipamorelin.

dosing.notes: Dose shown is Ipamorelin (daily). CJC-1295 DAC: 1000 mcg once weekly on a fixed day. Same protocol as 2mg version but 5mg vial provides 2.5× more doses.

storage.notes: Store vials separately; do not pre-mix

peptides.primaryUse: High-dose GH stack for advanced anti-aging and body recomposition

peptides.name: PT-141 + Melanotan II (10mg+10mg) Stack
peptides.description: Two-vial melanocortin stack. Melanotan II is used daily (loading → maintenance) for skin pigmentation and sexual function. PT-141 (Bremelanotide) is used on-demand for sexual arousal, at least 45 minutes before activity. Two separate vials, separate purposes and schedules.

dosing.notes: Dose shown is Melanotan II (daily, loading phase). PT-141 is on-demand: 750-1750 mcg ≥45 min before activity, max 8 doses/month. Melanotan II transitions to 2-3×/week maintenance after loading.

titration.note: MT-II: up to 1000 mcg/day then maintenance 2-3×/week

reconstitution.notes: Both vials: 3mL BW → ~3.33mg/mL (33.3mcg/unit). Reconstituted MT-II: use within ~1 week.

storage.lyophilized: Freeze at -20°C (-4°F); PT-141 stable ~3 weeks at room temp
storage.notes: Protect from light; reconstituted MT-II degrades faster than PT-141

peptides.primaryUse: Combined sexual enhancement and tanning stack with melanocortin synergy

peptides.name: TB-500 (5mg) + BPC-157 (5mg) Stack
peptides.fullName: TB-500 5mg Vial + BPC-157 5mg Vial Stack Protocol
peptides.description: Classic two-vial tissue repair stack. TB-500 is dosed twice weekly (loading) then once weekly (maintenance) for systemic healing. BPC-157 is dosed daily for localized repair. Two separate vials with different schedules — synergistic tissue recovery through complementary mechanisms.

dosing.notes: Dose shown is BPC-157 (daily). TB-500: 1250 mcg 2×/week for wks 1-4, then 1250 mcg 1×/week for wks 5-12. BPC-157: 250 mcg/day wks 1-2, 500 mcg/day wks 3-8, 250 mcg/day wks 9-12.

reconstitution.notes: Both vials: 2mL BW → 2.5mg/mL (25mcg/unit on U-100). Reconstituted solutions usable ~30 days.

peptides.primaryUse: Comprehensive injury recovery stack targeting systemic and local healing
